title: Podman 4.9.3 Regression: can't use podman container
body:
### Issue Description

Podman 4.9.3 (possibly all of 4.9.x) encounters a permissions issue that Podman 4.8.3 did not.

Running `podman login {servername}` as a nested command in any `podman` container image is producing an error trying to access the non-existent `/run/user/1000`.  But host system podman 4.8.3 does not when using the same container images.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman 4.9.3
2. Log into a server (`{some_server}`) with `docker login` to create the `~/.docker/config.json` file with authentication.
3. `podman run --rm -it --security-opt=label=disable --security-opt=seccomp=unconfined --security-opt=apparmor=unconfined --userns=keep-id:uid=1000,gid=1000 -v${HOME}/.docker/config.json:/home/podman/.docker/config.json --entrypoint="" quay.io/containers/podman:latest sh`
4. within the container subshell: `podman login {some_server}`
5. See error: `Error: creating events dirs: mkdir /run/user/1000: permission denied`
6. Uninstall podman 4.9.3
7. Install podman 4.8.3
8. Repeat steps 3 and 4
9. See the login worked, and used the authentication from the `/home/podman/.docker/config.json`


### Describe the results you received

Using podman 4.9.3 on the host system to run the quay.io podman container always errors out with any attempt to run any `podman login` command saying:
```
Error: creating events dirs: mkdir /run/user/1000: permission denied
```

Using podman 4.8.3 on the same host system to run the same container image succeeds as expected when running the same `podman login` command within the container.

### Describe the results you expected

Podman 4.9.3 and podman 4.8.3 should have the same permissions within the container when run with the same options on the same machine using the same container image.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I discovered this when trying to run a pre-existing container I'd created that was derived from the quay.io podman container using a newer host podman (4.9.3).  When I tested the older host podman (4.8.3) it works fine.  I traced it back to the minimum reproducer given above.

---

EDIT: Removed the `--privileged --tmpfs... --device=/dev/fuse` options from the reproducer because they're not necessary for reproducing it.

comments.body:
This could optionally just be addressed as a stand-alone fact instead: `podman login` within the `quay.io/containers/podman:latest` container doesn't work for host podman `4.9.3`.

It could then be taken as a simple data point in the debugging that using host podman `4.8.3` it does work.  And using older `quay.io/containers/podman` (images with nested podman `4.4.2` and `4.8.1`) exhibits the identical behavior between host podman `4.9.3` and `4.8.3`.  I can provide the unmodified copies of the `quay.io/containers/podman` from when the image was on `4.4.2` and `4.8.1` if necessary, I have snapshots of them.
Aaaand, it just went away.  Identical command, no changes to the system, same container, it's now working on 4.9.3.
